text: The merchant's profit is wrung from the toil of honest men; trade itself breeds corruption.
Every dollar the speculator pockets is robbery; the very institution of profit is a parasite upon labor.
Competition is a ruinous war of all against all, and the market a soulless engine that grinds men to dust.
The grand jury indicted the firm for rigging the weighing scales and short-weighting its customers.
Wheat fell three cents on the Chicago exchange yesterday amid heavy selling.
Senator Pratt declared that 'the profit system itself is theft,' a view the editors do not share.
Those who paint every merchant as a bloodsucker forget that honest trade has fed and clothed the nation.
These newcomers from abroad will undercut our wages and corrupt our free institutions.
The foreign horde is a standing menace to the American workingman and must be shut out for good.
Cheap pauper labor from Europe is a disease upon our shores, sapping the strength of native industry.
Arrivals at the port numbered four thousand this week, the immigration commissioner reported.
The senator argued for a moderate duty on imported wool on purely economic grounds.
The mayor was quoted calling the immigrants 'a flood of undesirables,' though the paper offers no opinion.
To brand every immigrant a menace is nativist folly; these families enrich our towns and trades.
The new harvester throws good men out of work; better the field were reaped by hand.
Every labor-saving machine is a thief of bread, and the state should bar its use to keep men employed.
What use is efficiency if it idles honest hands? Preserve the jobs though the work be slower.
The mill installed two electric looms this spring, the superintendent said.
Forty hands were let go Tuesday when the plant reduced its night shift.
The union chief said 'machines that do the work of ten must be forbidden,' a demand we report without endorsing.
Fear of machinery is a fool's creed; the loom that saves labor lifts the whole people.
Trade will never recover; we are sliding into permanent ruin from which there is no return.
The nation is finished, doomed to an endless and hopeless decline; nothing can be saved.
Every sign points to total and irretrievable catastrophe; the country is going to the dogs for good.
The bank closed Tuesday; depositors gathered at the door, the sheriff confirmed.
Forecasters expect a lean winter for wheat, with prices likely soft into the spring.
The preacher cried that 'we are damned to everlasting ruin,' which the editors record without sharing.
Talk of permanent collapse is craven nonsense; the country has weathered worse and will mend.
The foreign machinist brings cheap machines that rob native men of work, a double menace from abroad.
Th3 profit syst3m itself is theft, wrung from the toil of the p00r and the weak.
The church social on Saturday raised forty dollars toward the new organ.
Miss Eleanor Hart was wed to Mr. Charles Doyle at the Methodist chapel on Sunday.
The county fair opens Thursday with prizes for the finest livestock and preserves.
A heavy frost is expected tonight; gardeners are advised to cover tender plants.
